title: Place Clinics Before Queues Explode
seed_problem: capacitated facility location with nearest-assignment (k-median style)
mutation: composite: budget-constrained facility SELECTION coupled to a load-BALANCING assignment, scored by travel cost plus an M/M/1-style queueing-delay term lambda_f/(mu_f-lambda_f) that is superlinear near saturation -- turning a plain k-median distance minimization into a distance-vs-congestion trade-off.
strategies: trivial: open the single largest-capacity affordable site and funnel every demand node to it (reproduces roughly the checker's budget-blind nearest-facility baseline order of magnitude, ignores distance and load entirely)

naive greedy: open sites cheapest-cost-first while budget allows, then route every demand node to its geometrically nearest open site, ignoring load -- overloads cheap central sites and can miss high-value sites entirely (needle/junk trap)

insight (strong): select sites by capacity-served per unit budget, then assign demand nodes (largest arrival rate first) to whichever open site minimizes travel cost PLUS the marginal increase in that site's queueing term -- naturally spreads load away from near-saturated sites even at extra travel distance
a general solver can go further with full local-search reassignment / an LP relaxation of the assignment coupled to a knapsack relaxation of the site selection
